title: 10 best herbal teas for stomach pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for stomach pain are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are known to soothe and relieve discomfort in the digestive system. These teas often include ingredients such as chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and licorice root, all of which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties that can help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach. By drinking these teas, individuals can find relief from symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and nausea, allowing them to go about their daily activities with greater comfort. For example, a person who frequently experiences stomach pain after meals may find that drinking a cup of chamomile tea before bed helps to alleviate their discomfort and improve their quality of sleep. Similarly, someone who suffers from motion sickness may find that sipping on ginger tea during travel helps to prevent nausea and vomiting. Overall, herbal teas can be a natural and effective way to manage stomach pain and improve one's overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with stomach pain because it contains menthol, a compound that has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce pain. Additionally, peppermint oil has been shown to improve the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), a common cause of stomach pain. The soothing warmth of the tea can also help to alleviate discomfort and promote relaxation. Furthermore, peppermint tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and natural option for those seeking relief from stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint tea helps relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing stomach pain and cramping caused by spasms. This property is due to the active constituent menthol.
Carminative: Peppermint tea aids in the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, alleviating bloating, discomfort, and pain. The active constituent, menthol, contributes to this carminative effect.
Analgesic: Peppermint tea has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the pain associated with stomach ailments. The active constituent, menthol, is responsible for this analgesic effect.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tea reduces inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help soothe and heal stomach pain. The active constituents, rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, contribute to this anti-inflammatory effect.
Antiemetic: Peppermint tea can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms associated with stomach pain. The active constituent, menthol, is responsible for this antiemetic effect.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol and methyl salicylate, which have antispasmodic and analgesic properties that can help relieve stomach pain.
Flowers: Rich in volatile oils, including menthol, that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Seeds: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves, adjusting for personal taste.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes to ensure it's not too hot.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for 5-10 minutes, covered.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, discarding them safely.
Sip the herbal peppermint tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with stomach pain because it contains antispasmodic properties that help relax the smooth muscles of the intestines, thereby reducing stomach cramps and pain. It has anti-inflammatory and carminative effects that help soothe the stomach lining, alleviate inflammation, and expel gas, making it an effective remedy for various stomach ailments such as indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome. Furthermore, its calming and sedative properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to stomach discomfort, providing overall relief and promoting digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol and matricin, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thus alleviating pain.
Spasmolytic: Chamomile tea contains spasmolytic agents, such as flavonoids, that help relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing stomach cramps and pain.

Carminative: German chamomile tea has carminative properties, due to volatile oils like chamazulene and alpha-bisabolol, which help expel gas from the digestive tract, providing relief from bloating and pain.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of chamomile tea, attributed to its polyphenols and flavonoids, can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the likelihood of pain and discomfort.

Anxiolytic: German chamomile tea has anxiolytic effects, primarily due to the apigenin content, which may help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This can indirectly help alleviate stomach pain caused by stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, making them effective in soothing stomach pain.

Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain volatile oils, including alpha-bisabolol, which has been shown to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate stomach discomfort.

Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile are a rich source of essential oils, such as chamazulene, which has been found to have gastroprotective and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the chamomile flowers, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Cover the teapot or cup to retain the volatile oils from the chamomile flowers.
Strain the tea and discard the spent flowers.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for stomach pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with stomach pain because it has antispasmodic properties that help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing painful spasms and cramping. Additionally, lemon balm contains compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help to alleviate pain and discomfort. Furthermore, lemon balm has been shown to have calming and stress-reducing effects, which can help to reduce tension and anxiety that can exacerbate stomach pain. Drinking lemon balm tea can provide a soothing and natural remedy for stomach pain, promoting digestive health and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Lemon balm tea helps to relieve stomach pain by reducing bloating and gas, due to the presence of volatile oils like citronellal and citral.
Spasmolytic: It helps to alleviate stomach pain by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, owing to the antispasmodic compound rosmarinic acid.
Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea reduces inflammation in the stomach lining, which can cause pain, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Anxiolytic: By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, lemon balm tea can help alleviate stomach pain caused by stress or nervousness, thanks to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the stomach, preventing infections that can lead to pain and discomfort, due to its citral and eugenol content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea due to their soothing properties that can help alleviate stomach pain.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea because they contain antispasmodic properties that can help reduce stomach cramps and pain.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant are occasionally used in medicinal tea due to their ability to help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and add 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discard the leaves, and enjoy the soothing beverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with stomach pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thus alleviating pain. It is also rich in antioxidants that can help protect the stomach from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, white tea has been shown to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, cramping, and nausea. The soothing effects of white tea make it an ideal beverage for individuals experiencing stomach discomfort. Its gentle nature allows it to be consumed on an empty stomach without causing further irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stomach pain by decreasing inflammation in the digestive tract, thanks to the presence of flavonoids like catechins.
Antioxidant: White tea contains potent antioxidants that protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing stomach pain and discomfort, due to high levels of polyphenols.

Antispasmodic: White tea has antispasmodic properties that help alleviate stomach pain by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles and reducing cramping, which is attributed to volatile oils and tannins.

Antibacterial: White tea exhibits antibacterial properties that can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach, such as H. pylori, which is known to cause stomach pain and ulcers, because of its rich content of polyphenols and caffeine.

Carminative: White tea acts as a carminative, helping to relieve stomach pain by expelling gas from the digestive tract and preventing bloating, due to the presence of essential oils like limonene and linalool.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate pain.
Buds: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce pain.
Stems: May contain compounds that can help regulate digestion and alleviate stomach pain.
Flowers: Can have calming effects on the stomach and may help reduce stomach pain and discomfort.
Roots: May have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 175-185°F (80-85°C), as boiling water can damage the delicate properties of white tea.
Place 1 teaspoon (2-3 grams) of dried herbal white tea (such as peppermint, chamomile, or ginger) in a teapot or infuser.
Pour the hot water over the tea leaves and let them steep for 3-5 minutes, depending on the desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the spent tea leaves.
Drink the herbal white tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, to soothe stomach pain and promote digestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with stomach pain because it contains glycyrrhizic acid, a compound that can help soothe and protect the mucous lining of the stomach. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing stomach pain due to acid reflux, inflammation, or stomach ulcers. Additionally, licorice tea has been shown to increase the mucus production in the gastrointestinal tract, which can provide a further layer of protection for the stomach lining. Furthermore, licorice tea has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the stomach muscles and alleviate cramping and pain. Overall, licorice tea can be a natural and effective way to find relief from stomach pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is important for stomach pain as it can alleviate symptoms of conditions such as gastritis and stomach ulcers. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Mucosal protective: Licorice tea can help protect the stomach lining by stimulating the production of mucin, a substance that forms a protective barrier against stomach acid. This property is important for stomach pain as it can prevent and heal stomach ulcers and reduce acid reflux. The active constituent responsible for this property is glycyrrhizin.

Antispasmodic: Licorice tea can relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for stomach pain as it can alleviate symptoms of cramping, bloating, and gas. The active constituent responsible for this property is glycyrrhetic acid.

Demulcent: Licorice tea has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, which is important for stomach pain as it can provide relief from irritation and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are polysaccharides and flavonoids.

Antacid: Licorice tea can neutralize stomach acid, which is important for stomach pain as it can provide relief from symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn. The active constituent responsible for this property is glycyrrhizin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for stomach pain due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat stomach pain, as they contain flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation.

Seeds: Licorice tea seeds are used in medicinal teas for stomach pain due to their ability to stimulate the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice tea plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-ulcer effects, making it useful in treating stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root and place it in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the licorice root.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain out the licorice root.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for stomach pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with stomach pain because it contains volatile oils and compounds such as fenchone, estragole, and anethole, which have antispasmodic and carminative properties. These properties help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce inflammation, allowing for the relief of stomach cramps, bloating, and gas. Additionally, fennel tea has been shown to increase the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food and promote healthy digestion. Its soothing and mildly sweet flavor also makes it a pleasant and comforting drink for those experiencing stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel tea can help to relieve stomach pain by reducing bloating and gas, due to the presence of volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.
Antispasmodic: It can help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing pain and cramping, thanks to the presence of compounds like estragole and fenchone.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating pain and discomfort, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Diuretic: It can help to flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body, reducing bloating and discomfort, due to the presence of potassium and other diuretic compounds.
Digestive stimulant: Fennel tea can help to improve digestion and nutrient absorption, reducing stomach pain and discomfort, due to the presence of volatile oils and other digestive stimulants.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for stomach pain due to their carminative and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant are used in tea to soothe stomach pain because they contain volatile oils that help relax gastrointestinal muscles and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes are rich in anethole, a compound that has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for stomach pain.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in tea to alleviate stomach discomfort and promote digestion.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Crush or grind 1-2 teaspoons of fennel seeds and add them to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the fennel seeds and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tea helps with stomach pain because it contains carvol and carvene, two compounds known for their antispasmodic and carminative properties. These properties help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing cramps and spasms, and relieving bloating and gas. Additionally, caraway tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to break down food more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of stomach discomfort. Furthermore, caraway tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal irritated stomach lining, providing relief from pain and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Caraway tea helps to relieve stomach pain by reducing bloating and gas, due to the active constituents carvol and carvone which promote the expulsion of intestinal gas.
Antispasmodic: It alleviates stomach pain by relaxing gastrointestinal muscles, thanks to the compound cuminaldehyde that helps reduce smooth muscle spasms.
Anti-inflammatory: Caraway tea can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing stomach pain; this is attributed to the presence of limonene, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Digestive stimulant: It encourages the production of digestive enzymes, improving nutrient absorption and reducing stomach discomfort; this is facilitated by the active constituent carvone.
Anesthetic: Caraway tea has a mild numbing effect, which can help alleviate pain in the gastrointestinal tract; this is attributed to the compound carvone, which has local anesthetic properties.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal tea for stomach pain due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Leaves: The leaves of the caraway tea plant are used in medicinal tea for stomach pain as they contain anti-spasmodic compounds that help to relax the gastrointestinal tract.
Rhyzomes: Caraway rhyzomes are often included in medicinal tea for stomach pain because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and heal the stomach lining.
Flowers: The flowers of the caraway tea plant are used in medicinal tea for stomach pain as they contain antioxidants that can help to protect the stomach from damage caused by free radicals.
Roots: Caraway roots are sometimes included in medicinal tea for stomach pain because they contain volatile oils that can help to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried caraway seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the caraway seeds.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with stomach pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and cramping. Additionally, spearmint has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat harmful bacteria in the stomach, further alleviating discomfort. Furthermore, the soothing warmth of the tea can help stimulate digestion and alleviate feelings of bloating and fullness. Overall, spearmint tea can provide natural relief for various types of stomach pain and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Spearmint tea helps to relieve stomach pain by reducing bloating and gas, due to the presence of menthol and volatile oils.
Antispasmodic: Spearmint tea can alleviate stomach pain by relaxing gastrointestinal muscles, thanks to the rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tea can soothe stomach pain by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, owing to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Spearmint tea can help manage stomach pain by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, due to the presence of carvacrol and limonene.
Digestive stimulant: Spearmint tea promotes stomach pain relief by enhancing the digestion process, owing to the presence of menthol and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Spearmint leaves are commonly used in medicinal tea for stomach pain due to their calming and antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds are sometimes included in medicinal tea for stomach pain because they may help reduce inflammation and promote digestion.
Stems: The stems of the spearmint plant can be used in medicinal tea for stomach pain, as they contain similar soothing compounds found in the leaves.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers are used less frequently, but they can be included in medicinal tea for stomach pain due to their mild anti-inflammatory and calming effects.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried spearmint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the spearmint leaves, ensuring a smooth drinking experience.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid milk or sugar to prevent masking the herb's benefits.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your stomach and alleviate pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tea helps with stomach pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and cramping. Additionally, mugwort has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and nutrient absorption, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas. Furthermore, mugwort tea may help regulate gut motility, preventing constipation and promoting regular bowel movements, thereby reducing the risk of stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Mugwort tea helps relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing painful spasms and cramps. This property is important for stomach pain because it can alleviate the discomfort caused by muscle spasms in the stomach. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as camphor and thujone.

Carminative: Mugwort tea promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating, cramps, and pain. This property is important for stomach pain because it can help relieve the pressure and discomfort caused by gas buildup in the stomach. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as cineole and borneol.

Cholagogue: Mugwort tea stimulates the production and flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, improving digestion and reducing stomach pain. This property is important for stomach pain because efficient digestion can help prevent stomach discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and bitter compounds.

Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort tea helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate pain and promote healing. This property is important for stomach pain because inflammation often contributes to stomach discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and terpenoids.

Anesthetic: Mugwort tea has a mild numbing effect, which can help alleviate pain in the digestive tract. This property is important for stomach pain because it can provide immediate relief from discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as thujone and camphor.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stem of mugwort contains many medicinal compounds, making it ideal for treating stomach pain.
Leaves: Rich in soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, mugwort leaves are often used to make medicinal tea for stomach pain.
Flowers: Mugwort flowers contain essential oils and other compounds that can help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
Seeds: Mugwort seeds are a potent source of medicinal compounds, including those that can help soothe an upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when you add the mugwort.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mugwort leaves to a tea infuser or a reusable tea bag.
Pour the hot water over the mugwort leaves and let them steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on how strong you want the tea.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag and discard the used mugwort leaves.
Drink the mugwort tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate stomach pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane tea helps with stomach pain because it contains compounds that can help soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract. It has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the stomach. Additionally, elecampane tea can help stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and reducing the likelihood of stomach discomfort. The tea's mild sedative effects can also help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can contribute to stomach pain. Overall, elecampane tea offers a natural and holistic approach to managing stomach pain and promoting digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elecampane tea helps reduce stomach inflammation, which is important for stomach pain as it is often caused by inflammation in the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties come from the plant's sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone.

Antispasmodic: Elecampane tea helps relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating painful spasms and cramps. This is due to the presence of alantolactone and isoalantolactone, which have antispasmodic effects.

Carminative: Elecampane tea aids in expelling gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort. The carminative property is attributed to the volatile oils found in elecampane, such as humulene and caryophyllene.

Antibacterial: Elecampane tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the stomach, which can contribute to pain and inflammation. The antibacterial properties are due to the presence of alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Mucilaginous: Elecampane tea forms a soothing, protective film over the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, helping to alleviate irritation and pain. This is due to the high mucilage content in elecampane roots.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elecampane roots are commonly used in medicinal tea for stomach pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and relax the digestive tract.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of elecampane are rich in various compounds, including inulin, which can aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of stomach pain.
Leaves: Elecampane leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat stomach pain, as they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract.
Flowers: Elecampane flowers are used in medicinal tea for stomach pain due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach and promote a healthy gut.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried elecampane root to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the elecampane root and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea slowly, up to three times a day, for stomach pain relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stomach pain with herbal teas is by selecting teas with soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Peppermint, ginger, chamomile, and licorice root teas are excellent choices. Peppermint and ginger help relax gastrointestinal muscles, reducing pain and inflammation. Chamomile has calming effects on the digestive system, while licorice root soothes and protects the stomach lining. Steep the tea in hot water for 5-10 minutes, then drink it slowly to allow the herbs to coat and ease the stomach. Remember to consult with a healthcare professional if symptoms persist or worsen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach pain that are treated with herbal teas include indigestion, bloating, and nausea. For instance, chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help soothe an upset stomach, while ginger tea is often used to alleviate feelings of nausea and vomiting. Peppermint tea is another popular choice, as it can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce bloating and gas. Licorice root tea is also used to treat stomach ulcers and heartburn. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment, especially for pregnant women and those with underlying medical conditions.
